summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to remote endpoint
details: On every `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js collects developer- and host-identifying information and POSTs it as JSON to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth file, which also contains the user's OAuth token), plus GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL/GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL/EMAIL environment variables, os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI provider detection. The transmitted payload includes scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, and cwd — directly identifying the installer despite README framing as 'fully anonymous diagnostics.' The package name and description impersonate Vercel's official @ai-sdk/* scope (description: 'Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK'; author hello@ai-sdk.guide is unaffiliated with Vercel), targeting developers searching for Vercel AI SDK utilities. Reading a credential store (gh hosts.yml) and silently shipping developer identity off-host on install is classic developer-recon for targeted follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
